title: Adder Circuits and the Arithmetic Logic Unit
summary: Covers half-adder, full adder, ripple-carry adder, carry lookahead adder, carry selection adder, and the 1-bit and 16-bit ALU.
content_points: Half-adder: Sum = A XOR B; Carry = A AND B. Cannot cascade directly.
Full adder: adds A, B, and carry-in; outputs Sum and Carry-out. Can be cascaded.
Ripple-carry adder: n full adders in series; simple but delay proportional to n.
Carry lookahead adder: C_i = G_i + P_i·C_{i-1}; computes all carries in parallel – high speed, complex circuitry.
Carry selection adder: computes both carry-in=0 and carry-in=1 results; selects correct one – compromise.
1-bit ALU performs AND, OR, ADD, or SUB based on 2 control bits (F1, F0).
learning_objectives: Design half-adder and full adder circuits
Explain carry propagation in a ripple-carry adder
Compare ripple-carry and carry lookahead adders
Describe the structure and operation of a simple ALU
